title: Cornbread with Corn
description: Classic buttermilk cornbread with whole corn kernels folded in. Bakes in a cast-iron skillet or baking dish in about 25 minutes.

ingredients.name: buttermilk (or milk + 1 tbsp lemon juice or vinegar)
unsalted butter, melted (plus extra for greasing)
corn kernels (fresh, canned and drained, or frozen and thawed)

instructions.text: Preheat oven to 400°F. Grease a 9-inch cast-iron skillet or 8x8-inch baking dish with butter.
In a large bowl, whisk together cornmeal, flour, baking powder, and salt.
In a separate bowl, whisk together buttermilk, eggs, and melted butter until smooth.
Pour wet ingredients into dry and mix until just combined -- do not overmix. Fold in corn kernels and any optional add-ins.
Pour batter into the prepared skillet or dish. Bake 20–25 minutes until the top is golden and a toothpick inserted in the center comes out clean.
Cool slightly before slicing. Serve warm with butter, honey, or alongside soups and stews.

notes:
No buttermilk? Add 1 tbsp lemon juice or white vinegar to 1 cup of milk, stir, and let sit 5 minutes.

Cast iron gives the best crust -- preheat the skillet in the oven for 5 minutes before adding the batter for an extra-crispy bottom.

Optional add-ins: shredded cheddar and diced jalapeño are a classic combination.
